MWCapture SDK  3.3.1.1556
  • Home
  • Development Library
  • Examples
  • Release Notes
XICaptureQuad C++

The features are as follows

  1. previewing videos from multiple channels in sync, support up to 4 channels.
  2. specifying the preview layout mode, video channels, resolutions, frame rate, sync mode etc.

The example is used to capture multiple channels simultaneously.

Folder dir: SDKv3\Examples\VC++\GUI\XICaptureQuad

Supported Hardware

Pro Capture Family

Features

  1. Preview videos from 4 channels in sync.
  2. Customize the preview layout mode, video channels, resolutions, frame rate, sync mode etc.

Workflow

The main thread:
  1. To initialize and enumerate all available devices, call: MWCaptureInitInstance, MWGetChannelCount
  2. To find the valid capture card according to the device info,, call: MWGetChannelInfoByIndex
  3. Choose the layout, capture devices, resolutions, frame rate, and whether to use the synchronized mode.
  4. To create and initialize a video renderer, call: MWDXRender,initialize
  5. To open the chosen video capture channel one by one, call: MWGetDevicePath, MWOpenChannelByPath
  6. To start a video capture, call: MWStartVideoCapture
  7. To register the notification for a cached frame, call: MWRegisterNotify
  8. Create process threads:
    1. If you tick the synchronized mode, a thread will be created for it.
    2. Create a video process thread.
  9. Enter the main thread loop.
  10. To free up system resources and memory after exiting the capture application, call: MWCloseChannel and MWCaptureExitInstance
The Video Capture Thread Workflow:
  1. To register a timer, call: MWRegisterTimer
  2. To capture a frame of video data triggered by the set timer, call: MWScheduleTimer, MWCaptureVideoFrameToVirtualAddressEx
  3. To get current capture status, and clear the memory of capture card, call: MWGetVideoCaptureStatus
  4. Render captured video.
The synchronized process thread:
  1. Wait for the notification that video capture ends.
  2. To get video InfoFrame, call: MWGetVideoFrameInfo
  3. Make the first fully stored frame as the reference and its capture duration as the reference time range.
  4. If the time when one frame is fully stored in the memory is within the reference time range for a channel, the frame is concurrent. Otherwise, the frame would be discarded. All captured frames of all channels will be compared with each reference, until the capture ends.
  5. Set a mask for each channel, which determined whether to capture videos or not.

Flow Chart

XICaptureQuad

Result

Seamless preview of quad-channel matrix
